[2018-09-26 12:55] darkeisbein: and what the term actually means differs greatly from game to game and studio to studio
[2018-09-26 12:56] darkeisbein: to the point of each of those essentially being a released game
[2018-09-26 12:56] inuyaki: I never heard anyone saying a game is released once it is in beta
[2018-09-26 12:57] inuyaki: Well, until 1min ago that is
[2018-09-26 12:57] darkeisbein: but it happens all the time
[2018-09-26 12:57] darkeisbein: there totally are games that essentially had a full release and called it open beta
[2018-09-26 12:58] darkeisbein: and there are also games that treat there open beta as an actual beta
[2018-09-26 12:58] darkeisbein: but how is one supposed to know what kind of game they are dealing with?
[2018-09-26 12:59] inuyaki: Because there are a few outliers who are wishy washy... nice argument
[2018-09-26 12:59] inuyaki: If a game is in Early Access, it is not released... except the DEVELOPER tells you otherwise
[2018-09-26 13:00] darkeisbein: the entire classification of beta, open beta, early acess, full release is wishy washy. The only objective fact is wether the game is available for pickup/purchase or not
[2018-09-26 13:01] darkeisbein: calling something a beta or a release doesn't really change anything about that thing, it's just part of the communication and marketing strategy
[2018-09-26 13:03] inuyaki: No, there are clear distinctions... closed beta = you have to be invited or buy your way in, open beta = everyone can participate, release = development is done and you can buy the game (or just start it in case of f2p)

[2018-09-26 13:04] inuyaki: btw Prismata will still be EA

[2018-09-26 13:05] inuyaki: Prismata will still be considered an Early Access product until we complete the rest of the campaign and launch globally.
[2018-09-26 13:05] inuyaki: From Elyots announcement

[2018-09-26 13:06] darkeisbein: is League of Legends released?

[2018-09-26 13:06] darkeisbein: how is league not in development?
[2018-09-26 13:07] inuyaki: Adding stuff != development
[2018-09-26 13:07] ruinedshadows: I think calling Prismata EA until the rest of the campaign and core single player content is finished is reasonable
[2018-09-26 13:07] ruinedshadows: but it doesn't really change the state of the game
[2018-09-26 13:07] darkeisbein: I mean yeah I don't disagree but it's equally reasonable to call it released
[2018-09-26 13:07] ruinedshadows: the game's multiplayer fundamentals are pretty solidified at this poitn
[2018-09-26 13:07] darkeisbein: because there is no inherent difference between early access and released
[2018-09-26 13:07] ruinedshadows: yeah the difference is meta stuff
[2018-09-26 13:08] ruinedshadows: like access to the game
[2018-09-26 13:08] ruinedshadows: and marketing/distribution/publication
[2018-09-26 13:08] inuyaki: Also stuff like bugs and testing
[2018-09-26 13:08] ruinedshadows: also, the game will not always be in development
[2018-09-26 13:08] darkeisbein: just calling it a certain name does nothing about bugs and testing
[2018-09-26 13:08] inuyaki: Elyot pushed a lot of unfinished translation stuff to test it... on the live system
[2018-09-26 13:08] ruinedshadows: elyot has said that lunarch has plans eventually for other games that prismata will be a mostly finished product
[2018-09-26 13:08] inuyaki: That would NEVER happen outside of EA
[2018-09-26 13:08] darkeisbein: because you call a game early access it doesn't magically have more bugs
[2018-09-26 13:08] inuyaki: which was his argument to do so btw "it is EA anyways"
[2018-09-26 13:09] ruinedshadows: (maybe occasionally rebalances or adding new units)
[2018-09-26 13:09] inuyaki: You would never push stuff to test on a released game...
[2018-09-26 13:09] inuyaki: Except you are Blizzard maybe... but let's not go down there
[2018-09-26 13:09] ruinedshadows: yeah I agree with inuyaki on that point, the game is still EA because money is not being spent on selling the game and publicizing it as much, more of it is being spent on development and testing within the small community that does exist today

[2018-09-26 13:30] lavenders2: Buying units individually is way too expensive
[2018-09-26 13:30] ludokultur (Fabian Fischer): "Nothing too interesting or new." combined with "if card games are your thing then it might be for you" really suggests they didn't get the game at all 😕
[2018-09-26 13:31] inuyaki: So "cosmetics are too expensive" is a valid point for a bad review nowadays?
[2018-09-26 13:31] lavenders2: I mean yeah its part of the product
[2018-09-26 13:32] inuyaki: This has nothing to do with a game review imo
[2018-09-26 13:32] lavenders2: It may not be important to some people but it is a valid complaint
[2018-09-26 13:32] awaclus: it's also a matter of taste
[2018-09-26 13:33] inuyaki: Prices are always high for f2p games... so you know they can be f2p for everyone who does not wanna have those cosmetics
[2018-09-26 13:33] awaclus: I guess for some people it's fun to get to customize your stuff, but for me, the important part is to achieve and show off the ability to do so rather than actually customize
[2018-09-26 13:34] awaclus: which means that if people were able to buy like everything for $1, that would make it pointless for me to grind for some skin or whatever
[2018-09-26 13:35] lavenders2: I mean the ability to customize your units is where almost all of prismatas money is supposed to be coming from
[2018-09-26 13:36] inuyaki: Prismata gives a lot of free stuff now with the Road to Glory stuff... I mean you can complain that you find the cosmetics too expensive... but not in a game review imo -.-
[2018-09-26 13:36] inuyaki: well, we can agree to disagree here... other valid points?
[2018-09-26 13:38] lavenders2: I guess beyond monetization no
[2018-09-26 13:39] lavenders2: But apart from that the guy says its a game built for pvp and that aspect is fun
[2018-09-26 13:39] awaclus: and proceeds to give it a 5 - Mediocre
[2018-09-26 13:39] lavenders2: Well he even said he didnt really review it from a pvp players perspective
[2018-09-26 13:40] lavenders2: And that it was difficult to score because of that
[2018-09-26 13:41] awaclus: when you are reviewing a product, you should at least make an effort to review how good it is, not how much you personally like it
[2018-09-26 13:41] lavenders2: Reviews are still opinion pieces and should be taken with a grain of salt
[2018-09-26 13:42] awaclus: that doesn't mean the writer shouldn't have any responsibilities
[2018-09-26 13:42] lavenders2: I feel the main reason he might have docked it is because the game didnt offer anything interesting or new to him
[2018-09-26 13:43] lavenders2: What responsibilities?
[2018-09-26 13:43] awaclus: like making an effort to review how good it is, not how much you personally like it
[2018-09-26 13:44] darkeisbein: I feel the reviewer honestly tried to do that and just missed the part that multiplayer and bot skirmishes are actually deep
[2018-09-26 13:44] lavenders2: I mean that is hard and is why the big review sites have more than one reviewer
[2018-09-26 13:44] awaclus: if I'd written that in literature class, I'd gotten a terrible grade for it
[2018-09-26 13:44] awaclus: simply on the basis of subjectivity
[2018-09-26 13:45] awaclus: also on the basis of how good the writing was
[2018-09-26 13:45] inuyaki: 2 of his bad points are "The grind for currency is terrible" and "Too pricey at release" and a 3rd is "Main protagonist unlikable" in a game that is mostly pvp and even that is subjective, because yes he was supposed to be an asshole but I liked it about him
[2018-09-26 13:45] lavenders2: Isnt subjectivity just that? Isnt that like saying you will give someone a bad grade because you didnt agree with their opinion
[2018-09-26 13:45] inuyaki: And the 4th is "Nothing too interesting or new." which he cannot fathom after not having it really played
[2018-09-26 13:46] lavenders2: Even if that opinion goes heavily against the grain or is controversial
[2018-09-26 13:46] awaclus: >Isnt subjectivity just that? Isnt that like saying you will give someone a bad grade because you didnt agree with their opinion
well yes, that's what the review is doing
[2018-09-26 13:47] inuyaki: That's like rating a shooter bad because "I didn't like the hairstyle of my character"
[2018-09-26 13:47] inuyaki: Yes it can annoy you and maybe you don't like the game because of it, but that has NOTHING to do with game review
[2018-09-26 13:47] lavenders2: Its still subjective, its why i dont like scores in reviews to be honest
[2018-09-26 13:48] lavenders2: Like if he had no score and just left his review as it was
[2018-09-26 13:48] lavenders2: It honestly wouldnt be an awful review
[2018-09-26 13:48] awaclus: yeah if it didn't have a score, that in and of itself would make it a way better review
[2018-09-26 13:49] lavenders2: So the number changed your opinion of the review
[2018-09-26 13:49] lavenders2: And not the content of the review itself
[2018-09-26 13:49] inuyaki: > Final Thoughts
It has a nice premise with a good element of gameplay involved. It’s just the fact that monetization and the campaign weigh it down. It’s $25, which doesn’t feel worth it. Maybe if you were to catch it on sale for more than half that then sure. It’s a solid PvP game, but that’s all it is meant for. It’s not even that immersive of a game to keep playing regularly. For some it might be, but for others it’s just a casual game.

This in itself is really bad
[2018-09-26 13:50] lavenders2: Well yea cus that actually has what is about to be factually false info
[2018-09-26 13:51] awaclus: >So the number changed your opinion of the review
>and not the content of the review itself
The number is a part of the content of the review itself
[2018-09-26 13:51] inuyaki: Again 3 of his main bad points were costs and main char is unlikable ... "It’s a solid PvP game, but that’s all it is meant for."
[2018-09-26 13:51] awaclus: it's fair to say that you don't like something about the game, but saying that the game is a 5 - mediocre because it has the things that you personally didn't like is not
[2018-09-26 13:52] lavenders2: Well he didnt like those things so he gave it a bad score, again its why i dont like scores
[2018-09-26 13:52] awaclus: >Well he didnt like those things so he gave it a bad score
which is what he shouldn't have done
[2018-09-26 13:53] awaclus: as a reviewer, he should have been able to look past his personal preferences and come to a more objective conclusion about the quality of the game

[2018-09-29 20:36] overmasterp: I added a lot of Tarsiers too
[2018-09-29 20:36] ruinedshadows: if there is a big absorber, you usually want to build the mist efficient permanent attack

[2018-09-29 20:36] mrguy888: so just learn 1 thing per game

[2018-09-29 20:38] mrguy888: 😭 is literally crying
[2018-09-29 20:38] amalloy: @OvermasterP you can make use of mrguy's suggestion right away if you want, by playing the set you just lost against master bot, and trying the thing that beat you

[2018-09-29 20:39] swapgo: And how practical it is to get it out

Etc

[2018-09-29 20:40] ruinedshadows: yeah those are complicated
[2018-09-29 20:40] overmasterp: A lot of attackers sre qualified like that is all
[2018-09-29 20:41] overmasterp: Which can make it hard to build efficiently if you don't know any better
[2018-09-29 20:41] overmasterp: It adds a lot of depth tho
[2018-09-29 20:41] ruinedshadows: yeah, that does make it more complicated for them to work
[2018-09-29 20:41] ruinedshadows: so typically you'll need to figure out what other benefits they have
[2018-09-29 20:42] medar: Yeah, the units that are harder to get out/akward to use are generally better, but you have to evaluate if it's worth it
[2018-09-29 20:42] ruinedshadows: like with perforater for example, you get to sink your extra red into attack if you want or you can use it as a blocker that just gives you threat since it is rather cheap
[2018-09-29 20:42] medar: Units that have some special upside, like granade mech burst damage, are generally bad unless you can make good use of that upside
[2018-09-29 20:43] ruinedshadows: definitely the units have a lot of complexities that take time to learn
[2018-09-29 20:43] ruinedshadows: but in general, if you look at the cost to attack ratio, you can see what will be the most efficient attack
[2018-09-29 20:43] ruinedshadows: a lot of times that is enough to win
